title: API Key Management CLI

story.i_want_to: create, list, revoke, and rotate API keys via the CLI
story.so_that: I can manage access credentials for my agents, orchestrators, and team members without making raw API calls

rationale: API key management is essential for tenant administrators who need to provision keys for different agents and roles, audit which keys exist, revoke compromised keys, and rotate keys on a schedule. The CLI provides a convenient interface to the key management API endpoints, making it easy to integrate key lifecycle management into scripts and automation workflows.

acceptance_criteria.description: 'loopctl keys create --name NAME --role ROLE' calls POST /api/v1/api_keys and displays the returned key details including the raw API key (shown only once)
'loopctl keys create' validates that --role is one of: user, orchestrator, agent; rejects invalid roles with a clear error message before making the API call
'loopctl keys list' calls GET /api/v1/api_keys and displays all keys with id, name, role, key_prefix, last_used_at, and expires_at (never the full key)
'loopctl keys revoke KEY_ID' calls DELETE /api/v1/api_keys/:id and confirms the key was revoked
'loopctl keys rotate KEY_ID' calls POST /api/v1/api_keys/:id/rotate and displays the new raw API key (shown only once) along with the grace period for the old key
All keys commands respect the --format flag (json, human, csv)
The create command includes a prominent warning that the raw API key is only displayed once and must be saved immediately
Keys list in human format displays as a formatted table with columns aligned
All keys commands require an authenticated session with a user-role API key; agent and orchestrator keys receive a 403 error

test_cases.name: Keys create sends correct payload and displays result
preconditions: Config file exists with valid server and api_key
Req.Test mock expects POST /api/v1/api_keys with {name: 'worker-1', role: 'agent'} and returns 201 with key data including raw_key
expected_results: POST request is sent with correct name and role
Output includes the raw API key from the response
Output includes a warning about saving the key

test_cases.name: Keys create rejects invalid role
expected_results: Returns {:error, message} indicating 'superadmin' is not a valid role for key creation
Error message lists valid roles: user, orchestrator, agent

test_cases.name: Keys list displays all keys in table format
preconditions: Config file exists with valid server and api_key
Req.Test mock returns 200 for GET /api/v1/api_keys with a list of 3 keys
expected_results: Output contains a table with columns: ID, Name, Role, Prefix, Last Used, Expires
Table shows 3 rows, one per key
No raw key values are displayed

test_cases.name: Keys revoke sends DELETE and confirms
preconditions: Config file exists with valid server and api_key
Req.Test mock expects DELETE /api/v1/api_keys/:id and returns 200
expected_results: Output confirms the key was revoked

test_cases.name: Keys rotate displays new key and grace period
preconditions: Config file exists with valid server and api_key
Req.Test mock expects POST /api/v1/api_keys/:id/rotate and returns 200 with new_key and grace_period_until
expected_results: Output includes the new raw API key
Output includes the grace period end time for the old key
Output includes a warning to save the new key

test_cases.name: Keys list in JSON format returns raw JSON array
preconditions: Config file exists with valid server and api_key
Req.Test mock returns 200 for GET /api/v1/api_keys with a list of keys
expected_results: Output is valid JSON that can be parsed by Jason.decode!/1
JSON contains an array of key objects with id, name, role, key_prefix fields

technical_notes: Role validation should happen client-side before the API call to give faster feedback; the valid roles for creation are user, orchestrator, and agent (superadmin keys can only be created through other means)
The raw API key from create and rotate responses should be highlighted or formatted prominently in human output mode
Keys list should handle pagination if the API supports it, but for v1 a simple single-page list is sufficient
The revoke command should accept the key UUID, not the key prefix, to avoid ambiguity
Use Req.Test.expect/3 to mock HTTP responses in tests rather than hitting a real server

Testing infrastructure: (1) All tests use async: true via DataCase/ConnCase, (2) Mox mocks defined in test/support/mocks.ex, (3) Default permissive stubs set in DataCase/ConnCase setup via stub_all_defaults/0 and Mox.set_mox_from_context(tags) for async isolation, (4) setup :verify_on_exit! in every test module, (5) Fixtures in test/support/fixtures.ex with build/2 (no DB) and fixture/2 (DB insert with auto-dependency resolution), (6) NEVER Application.put_env in tests — all service swapping via config/test.exs.

CLI HTTP client uses Req with Req.Test plug injection for tests. Config: config :loopctl, :api_req_plug, {Req.Test, Loopctl.CLI.Client} in test.exs. CLI client checks Application.get_env(:loopctl, :api_req_plug) and applies plug if present.
